params.name: Bronze Bull Head
params.description: This bronze bull head from the times of Shang and Zhou dynasties was unearthed at a heritage site in Jinsha, Chengdu. Its forehead is marked with a diamond pattern, the horns are covered in spirals, and the hollow neck can be hooked up to the bronze vessel. It is speculated to be a standing decoration on a large bronze vessel during the Shang and Zhou dynasties, and is of great significant value to the study of bronzeworking techniques in ancient Shu.
